page.title: Sunset Photoshoot on a Yacht: Marbella's Best Backdrops at Sea

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella sunset photoshoot yacht session from Puerto Banús. Capture golden hour with 12m+ yachts, champagne & La Concha views from €749.
data.summary: Combine a luxury yacht charter with a professional sunset photoshoot in Marbella. Depart from Puerto Banús for golden-hour backdrops of La Concha, the Costa del Sol coastline, and the Mediterranean.
key_facts: Charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella, with sunset times around 20:30–21:30 (May–Sep).
Choose from 12+ yachts, including Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) and Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h with jet ski).
Every charter includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (cava, beer, soft drinks), snacks, and IVA.
Best photo backdrops: La Concha mountain, Marbella coastline, Cabopino dunes, and open-sea golden light.
2-hour sunset charters are ideal for a quick photoshoot; longer bookings allow Cala del Faro or Estepona stops.
Photographer not included in base price; we can recommend local professionals upon request.
Maximum pax range from 5 (Dubhe) to 12 (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94).
All yachts are fully insured and equipped with safety gear for sunset cruising.

faq.q: What is the best time for a sunset photoshoot on a yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: Sunset in Marbella varies by season. From May to September, golden hour typically runs from 20:00 to 21:30, with the sun setting around 21:00. We recommend a 2-hour charter starting 90 minutes before sunset to capture the changing light over La Concha and the Mediterranean.

faq.q: Can I bring my own photographer on a sunset yacht charter?
faq.a: Yes, absolutely. Our charters are private, so you can bring a professional photographer or a friend to take photos. If you need a recommendation for a local photographer experienced in yacht shoots, just ask when booking. The photographer counts towards the maximum pax limit.

faq.q: Which yacht is best for a sunset photoshoot in Marbella?
faq.a: For a small group, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h, 9 pax) offers a sleek flybridge and open bow for clean shots. For larger groups or a luxury feel, the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h, 12 pax) provides a spacious main deck and a jet ski for action shots. Both depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is included in the sunset charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). You just need to bring your camera, outfit, and photographer.

faq.q: Are there any restrictions on photography equipment on board?
faq.a: No, you can bring cameras, tripods, drones (subject to local drone regulations), and lighting equipment. We only ask that equipment is used safely and does not obstruct the crew. The skipper can advise on the best spots for drone take-offs.

faq.q: What backdrops can I expect during a Marbella sunset photoshoot?
faq.a: Expect panoramic views of La Concha mountain, the Golden Mile coastline, Puerto Banús marina, and the open sea. Depending on the route, you may also capture Cabopino dunes, Cala del Faro cliffs, or the Estepona coastline. The light turns warm gold and pink over the water.

faq.q: Can I extend the charter if we want more time for photos?
faq.a: Yes, you can extend the charter on the day, subject to availability. A 2-hour sunset charter is popular for a quick shoot, while a 4-hour booking allows time to cruise to Cala Cortés or Estepona for varied backdrops. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange.

faq.q: How do I book a sunset photoshoot yacht session in Marbella?
faq.a: You can book directly on our website or message us on WhatsApp. Choose your preferred yacht and time slot. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak season (June–August). Payment is secure, and we confirm your skipper and drinks selection.

data.body_html:
Marbella's sunset is a spectacle of gold, pink, and orange that paints the Mediterranean and the Sierra Blanca mountains. A Marbella sunset photoshoot yacht session combines this natural beauty with the luxury of a private charter from Puerto Banús. Whether you're a couple, a family, or a content creator, our fleet of 20+ yachts offers the perfect platform for stunning imagery—all with champagne, snacks, and a licensed skipper included.

Why Choose a Yacht for Your Sunset Photoshoot in Marbella?

Land-based sunset spots in Marbella are often crowded, especially along the Golden Mile. A yacht gives you exclusive access to the open sea, where the horizon is uninterrupted and the light reflects off the water without buildings or cars in the frame. You control the angle, the speed, and the backdrop—from the silhouette of La Concha to the distant lights of Estepona. Plus, with the boat moving slowly, you can capture multiple compositions in one charter.

Best Yachts for a Sunset Photoshoot Session

Our fleet includes vessels from 6.4m to 29m, all departing from Puerto Banús. For a compact, intimate shoot, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) offers a clean flybridge and open bow at €749 for 2 hours—perfect for golden-hour portraits. For larger groups or a more luxurious feel, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) provides a spacious main deck and a jet ski for dynamic shots, starting at €4,719 for 4 hours. All charters include drinks (cava, beer, soft drinks) and light snacks.

Top Backdrops for Your Marbella Sunset Photoshoot

  • La Concha Mountain: The iconic peak turns purple and orange at sunset. Best captured from the stern or flybridge of yachts like the Azimut 58 Flybridge.
  • Puerto Banús Marina: The luxury port with superyachts and designer boutiques provides a glamorous foreground. Ideal for a quick stop before heading out.
  • Cabopino Dunes: A natural park with sand dunes and pine trees, visible from the sea. Great for a contrast of nature and luxury.
  • Cala del Faro: A secluded cove with a lighthouse, about 20 minutes west of Puerto Banús. The cliffs and turquoise water make for dramatic wide shots.
  • Open Sea Horizon: Once you're a mile offshore, the 360-degree view of the Mediterranean is unmatched. The light is clean and soft.

What to Expect on a 2-Hour Sunset Charter

A 2-hour charter is the most popular choice for a Marbella sunset photoshoot yacht session. You'll meet your skipper at Puerto Banús, board your chosen yacht, and head west towards Cabopino or Estepona. The first 30 minutes are for settling in and enjoying drinks. The next hour is prime golden hour—your photographer can capture portraits, couple shots, or group photos against the changing sky. The final 30 minutes bring you back to port as the lights of Marbella begin to twinkle. All safety equipment and insurance are included.

Photography Tips for Your Yacht Session

Bring a polarizing filter to reduce glare on the water. Use a wide-angle lens (24mm or wider) to capture the full scene with the yacht and horizon. For portraits, a 50mm or 85mm lens with a wide aperture (f/1.8 or f/2.8) will blur the background beautifully. If you're using a drone, check local regulations—our skipper can advise on no-fly zones. And don't forget to capture the details: champagne glasses, the helm, and the boat name for authentic branding.

How to Book Your Sunset Photoshoot Yacht Session

Booking is straightforward. Visit our Boat Rental Marbella homepage to browse the fleet and select your preferred yacht. For a sunset session, we recommend the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (both €749/2h) for small groups, or the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h) for a premium experience. You can also message us on WhatsApp for custom requests, such as adding a photographer or catering. We confirm availability within hours and send you a secure payment link.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best time for a sunset photoshoot on a yacht in Marbella?

Sunset in Marbella varies by season. From May to September, golden hour typically runs from 20:00 to 21:30, with the sun setting around 21:00. We recommend a 2-hour charter starting 90 minutes before sunset to capture the changing light over La Concha and the Mediterranean.

Can I bring my own photographer on a sunset yacht charter?

Yes, absolutely. Our charters are private, so you can bring a professional photographer or a friend to take photos. If you need a recommendation for a local photographer experienced in yacht shoots, just ask when booking. The photographer counts towards the maximum pax limit.

Which yacht is best for a sunset photoshoot in Marbella?

For a small group, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h, 9 pax) offers a sleek flybridge and open bow for clean shots. For larger groups or a luxury feel, the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h, 12 pax) provides a spacious main deck and a jet ski for action shots. Both depart from Puerto Banús.

What is included in the sunset charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). You just need to bring your camera, outfit, and photographer.

Are there any restrictions on photography equipment on board?

No, you can bring cameras, tripods, drones (subject to local drone regulations), and lighting equipment. We only ask that equipment is used safely and does not obstruct the crew. The skipper can advise on the best spots for drone take-offs.

What backdrops can I expect during a Marbella sunset photoshoot?

Expect panoramic views of La Concha mountain, the Golden Mile coastline, Puerto Banús marina, and the open sea. Depending on the route, you may also capture Cabopino dunes, Cala del Faro cliffs, or the Estepona coastline. The light turns warm gold and pink over the water.

Can I extend the charter if we want more time for photos?

Yes, you can extend the charter on the day, subject to availability. A 2-hour sunset charter is popular for a quick shoot, while a 4-hour booking allows time to cruise to Cala Cortés or Estepona for varied backdrops. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange.

How do I book a sunset photoshoot yacht session in Marbella?

You can book directly on our website or message us on WhatsApp. Choose your preferred yacht and time slot. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak season (June–August). Payment is secure, and we confirm your skipper and drinks selection.
